theme: Why 2026 is the year of the European incentive pivot

linkedin.text:
2026 is the year Israeli companies are fully pivoting to Europe for incentive travel.

After 4 years of post-COVID recovery, post-October 2023 adjustments, and a maturing understanding of European market dynamics, here's what I'm seeing:

The shift is structural, not cyclical.

1. Budget efficiency: The euro/shekel exchange rate, combined with rising Asian destination costs, has made Europe 20-30% more attractive on a cost-per-experience basis in 2026.

2. Group comfort: Israeli travelers have matured in their European travel. Barcelona, Athens, Prague are almost "home." Companies are exploring Porto, Split, Seville, Valletta — and finding extraordinary ROI.

3. Regulatory environment: Post-COVID, European venues have reopened at competitive rates. Boutique hotels, private estates, and heritage venues that were unavailable in 2022 are now accessible.

4. Supplier relationships: After 16 years building a network across Europe, we're seeing our best pricing in years. Vendors who survived COVID appreciate reliable partners.

5. The marketing angle: European destinations photograph and share exceptionally well. In a world where employee-generated content is a recruitment tool, the right trip in the right city has brand value beyond the event itself.

Our projection: 85% of our 2026 production will be in Europe. Up from 65% in 2024.

If you're planning a team event for Q3/Q4 2026 — the European window is still open. But not for long.

📩 alon@upe.co.il | 🌐 upe.co.il

#EuropeanEvents #IncentiveTravel #CorporateEvents2026 #Israel #MICE #UproductionEvents #EventIndustry #CorporateTravel #EuropeTravel #EventProduction #BusinessTravel
